extra.message: User input `username` query parameter is directly interpolated into a SQL SELECT query via f-string. An attacker can inject SQL via e.g. `?username=" OR 1=1; --` to extract all user records.

All fields of the User model (name, username, password, address, email, contact) are directly interpolated into a SQL INSERT query via f-string without parameterization. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through any of these fields.
User input `username` query parameter is directly interpolated into a SQL DELETE query via f-string. An attacker can delete all records via e.g. `?username=" OR 1=1; --`.

User input `user.address` from the request body is directly interpolated into a SQL DELETE query without even quoting. This makes injection trivial — no quote breakout is needed. An attacker can supply `1 OR 1=1` as the address to delete all rows.

The raw JSON body from the request is passed directly as a MongoDB query object to `get_nosql_users()`. An attacker can inject NoSQL operators like `{"$where": "this.id > 0"}`, `{"id":{"$gt":0}}`, or `{"$or":[...]}` to query arbitrary records, bypassing any intended filter logic.

Passwords are hashed using MD5 (`md5(user.password.encode()).hexdigest()`). MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password hashing — it is fast to brute-force and vulnerable to collision attacks. A proper key derivation function like bcrypt or argon2 should be used instead.

cwe: CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort

extra.message: The POST /reset endpoint deletes the entire SQL and NoSQL database and reinitializes it, yet requires no authentication or authorization. Any unauthenticated user can wipe and reset all data at any time, causing permanent data loss.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The DELETE /user endpoint allows any unauthenticated client to delete arbitrary user records from both SQL and NoSQL databases. There is no authentication or authorization check to verify the caller has permission to delete users.

The PUT /user endpoint allows any unauthenticated client to insert arbitrary user records into both SQL and NoSQL databases. No authentication or authorization is required, enabling anyone to create accounts or inject malicious data.

The `run_sql_query` helper returns user records including the password hash in the response (via the SQL SELECT * query on line 182). When returning a single user, the function includes all columns. While the NoSQL `get_nosql_users` function strips passwords (line 172), the SQL path does not, exposing password hashes to any caller.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
